Dashboard Design · B2B · 2026

HOTEL HUB

Operations dashboardReal-time dataHotel Management
The Project

A B2B operations dashboard that replaces 8+ fragmented tools with one unified command centre — designed for hotel management teams who never stop moving.

RoleUX/UI Designer
Year2026
PlatformWeb · B2B SaaS
ToolsFigma · Research
Case Study
Scroll
Category
B2B Dashboard · SaaS
My Role
UX Research · UI Design · Data Viz
Timeline
10 weeks · 2026
Status
Prototype complete
01The Problem

Eight systems. Zero clarity.

Hotel operations teams are constantly switching between disconnected tools — reservations here, housekeeping there, revenue in a spreadsheet. The result is slow decisions made on stale data.

Problem

Fragmented systems

Check-in in one tool, room status in another, staff schedules in a WhatsApp group, revenue in an end-of-day email. No one has the full picture at any given moment.

Problem

Data always 15–30 min behind

Because information lives in separate systems that don't talk to each other, any "overview" is assembled manually and immediately out of date. Managers are reacting, not deciding.

Solution

One unified dashboard

All key metrics — occupancy, check-ins, room status, open tasks, revenue — surfaced in a single view, refreshed live, with role-based filters so each user sees exactly what they need.

02User Research

Three roles. Three different needs.

The dashboard serves three types of users, each with a fundamentally different relationship to the data. Getting the information architecture right meant understanding all three before touching Figma.

General Manager

Needs the full picture at the start of each day. KPIs, overnight incidents, the day ahead. Doesn't need granular room-level data.

Overview first

Front Desk

Working real-time: who's checking in now, which rooms are ready, which guests have requests. Needs speed — not analysis.

Speed first

Housekeeping Supervisor

Needs to assign and track room-cleaning tasks, flag maintenance issues, and update room status in real time. Mobile-first.

Tasks first
03Key Screens

Dashboard · Room Grid · Revenue

04Core Features

Everything a hotel needs.
Nothing it doesn't.

Live KPI strip

Occupancy rate, today's check-ins, RevPAR, and open maintenance issues are always visible at the top — no scrolling required to understand the state of the hotel right now.

Room grid with colour-coded status

Every room mapped as a tile. Green = occupied, blue = ready, amber = cleaning, red = maintenance hold. Tap any room for guest details, timeline, and open tasks.

Priority task queue

Housekeeping, maintenance, and front desk actions unified in one list. Assign staff, set deadlines, mark complete — all from the dashboard, without switching tools.

Revenue at a glance

7-day occupancy trend and current RevPAR in the overview. Drill down for full analytics: ADR, channel breakdown, and forecast vs actual — without needing a separate finance tool.

05Design Decisions

Dark sidebar. Light canvas. Functional colour.

Every visual decision in a B2B dashboard serves the user's ability to read information quickly and accurately under pressure. Nothing is decorative.

Dark sidebar

Near-black navigation creates a strong visual anchor that separates nav from content. Users always know where they are in the app — even after hours of use.

Soft grey canvas

The main area uses #f6f7f9 instead of pure white. Reduces eye strain during long shifts. White cards create hierarchy within the grey field — no borders needed.

Functional colour only

Green = Occupied / Good
Blue = Ready / Available
Amber = Needs attention
Red = Urgent / Blocked

Scan in 3 seconds

That's the target. A manager walking in from a meeting should be able to understand the state of the hotel — including any problems — in under 3 seconds of looking at the screen.

Try it yourself

See Hotel Hub live in Figma.

The full prototype covers all three user roles — General Manager, Front Desk, and Housekeeping — with interactive room status updates and full mobile layouts.
